behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is Git rename detection as surfaced by the CLI in a real repo/index/worktree. A mock diff/status payload can miss subprocess output format, rename-threshold behavior, and path quoting/encoding from actual `git` execution. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML call in behavior text, and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit baseline file, rename it, run the CLI against real repo state, and assert report has the file only under `renamed` with no corresponding `added`/`deleted` entries.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary-diff handling failures often arise from real subprocess byte streams, decoding, and actual Git binary markers, which in-process fakes can hide. Integration is insufficient if it bypasses real `git` + filesystem interaction. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no model-dependent quality judgment and no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, add/modify a real binary file (e.g., image bytes), run CLI, assert process exits successfully and output includes expected binary file count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is handling real Git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (exit codes/stderr semantics). Mocked command responses can miss real CLI/subprocess edge handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior does not involve AI/ML output or chained agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real empty git repo with no commits, execute CLI, and assert zero exit plus empty structured report on stdout (not an exception/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, requiring real Git history traversal and CLI execution. Module-only tests can validate counting logic but can still miss failures in history extraction/parsing from real git commands. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Programmatically build a repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified operations across commits, run CLI on that repo, and assert reported counts equal ground truth derived from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: The boundary is real Git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/filesystem + git-behavior dependent. Fakes often collapse staged/unstaged distinctions and can hide real mismatches. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not include AI/ML-based decisions or multi-step agent pipelines.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets in two scenarios (staged-only vs unstaged-only), run CLI for each, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
